content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES
OF
CHARLES P. SCULLY, INC.
300 MONTGOMERY STREET
SUITE 735
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94104-1909

CHARLES P. SCULLY (1915-1985)
DONALD C. CARROLL
CHARLES P. SCULLY, II

October 22, 1990

TELEPHONE
362-0241
AREA CODE 415


Mr. Jeevan S. Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O.Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: California Labor Federation, AFL-CIO -
COPE

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

     This correspondence is transmitted in
accord with our telephone conversation of October 22,
1990.

     Per our discussion I am providing for back-
ground information the March 21, 1988 Informal Advice
Letter from the Commission.

     I would direct your attention to Footnote 6
of the same which states,

     "It is our understanding that you do
not take the position that each member
of each affiliate is a separate contri-
butor subject to the contribution
limits of Section 85302. In other
situations the contribution limits
may apply to individual members of
local organizations, which are
affiliated with state or national
organizations, rather than to the
affiliates.     This will depend upon
whether the contributions from individual
members are earmarked for the state or
national organization. The advice in
this letter is based on the specific
assumption that the contributions
from each member are not earmarked
for the state or national organization."

     As I explained during our recent telephone
conversation the Constitution of the California Labor
Federation, AFL-CIO did not, at the time of the
enclosed Informal Advice Letter, specifically earmark

Mr. Jeevan A. Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission

Page Two
October 22, 1990

contributions from individual members of affiliated
labor organization vis-a-vis COPE payments.

As I explained the Federation's Constitution
is subject to amendment only during the Federation's
regular biennial Convention. The Federation recently
held its first regular Convention since passage of
Proposition 73.

During the recent Convention the Federation
amended its Constitution by addition of the following,

"Amounts accruing to the Standing
Committee on Political Education
reflect voluntary earmarked contri-
butions at 5¢ per month from
individual members of affiliated
unions and will be utilized, in
part or in whole, for contributions
to candidates for California legislative
and constitutional offices. No per
capita payment shall be made by or
on behalf of financial core members
of affiliated unions."

Affiliated local unions, and the members thereof,
have been advised of the foregoing change to the
Federation's Constitution.

It is our opinion that given the aforesaid
Amendment the individual members of affiliated local
unions are now separate contributors subject to applicable
contribution limits.

Given the foregoing it is our view that absent
some extraordinary additional voluntary contribution
by a member of an affiliated local union the members
of affiliated local unions are making a maximum contri-
bution of 60¢ per calendar year to the Federation's
sponsored Political Action Committee.

We believe that individual affiliated local
unions are acting as "intermediaries" for purposes
of the aforesaid 5¢ per month contribution due to the
fact that the local unions receive the contribution,
as part of the member's voluntary dues, and then
transmit the same on to the Federation's Sponsored
Political Action Committee. In campaign reports filed

Mr. Jeevan A. Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission

Page Three
October 22, 1990

subsequent to the current election cycle the Federation's
sponsored Political Action Committee plans on reporting
contributions received through each affiliated local union,
acting as intermediaries, with an annotation that the
total amount from each affiliated local union reflects,
per month, 5¢ from "X" number of members received during
the months subject to reporting in each campaign statement.
In accord with past practice each local union will be
listed.

     We would request informal advice confirming
the fact that the foregoing is acceptable or, in the
alternative, advice as to what other form of annotation
the Commission believes appropriate.

     As an aside I would provide you with background
information vis-a-vis the final sentence of the constitutional
provision quoted in full above, in particular that sentence
reading,

     "No per capita payment shall be
     made by or on behalf of financial
     core members of affiliated unions."

     In a union setting a "financial core member"
is an individual who refuses to join the local union,
but who, due to some form of union security clause, is
required to pay periodic fees to the union with exclusive
bargaining rights covering the individual's employment
position.  A progression of United States Supreme Court
decisions has defined what may and may not be charged
as a service fee by a local union to financial core
members.  It is the position of the Federation that it
is a purely voluntary association.  Given the voluntary
nature of the Federation it is the Federation's position
that individuals who are not voluntary members of affiliated
local unions shold not be permitted to pay the 25¢ per
month which accrues to the Federation's General Fund nor
should such financial core members be permitted to pay
the voluntary 5¢ per month contribution to the Federation's
sponsored Political Action Committee.

     Finally, I would note that the two officers
of the Federation's sponsored Political Action Committee
are the only individuals with discretionary authority to
determine what contributions will be made to which
candidates.  Accordingly, neither affiliated local

o.I :o -

":.o -

Mr. Jeevan A. Ahuja
Fair Political Practices Commission

Page Four
October 22, 1990

unions, nor the members of affiliated local unions, in
fact direct and/or control contributions made by the
Federation's sponsored Political Action Committee to
candidates.

Hopefully, the foregoing provides sufficient
pertinent and background information for purposes of
issuance of the requested informal advice. However,
should you required any additional information, please
do not hesitate to contact me by the means most convenient
to you.

Thank you.

Very truly yours,

LAW OFFICES OF
CHARLES P. SCULLY, INC.

By [illegible]
Charles P. Scully II

CPSII:bjs
OPE-3-AFL-CIO
Enclosure

cc: Mr. John F. Henning
Mr. Albin J. Gruhn
